title: New Korean Potato Bake
description: A unique fusion of Korean flavors with a classic potato bake. This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion.

ingredients.name: New potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: Choose a mix of yellow and red potatoes for a colorful presentation

ingredients.name: Creamy Korean chili paste (gochujang)
ingredients.notes: Adjust the level of spiciness to your liking

ingredients.name: Bake seasoning
ingredients.notes: Try using a Korean-inspired blend for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil to prevent overpowering the dish

ingredients.name: Crushed garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before crushing for a more intense flavor

ingredients.name: Chopped green onions
ingredients.notes: Use the green parts for added color and freshness

ingredients.name: Sesame seeds
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a nice nutty flavor and crunchy texture

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Adjust the level of saltiness to your liking

instructions: In a large bowl, toss the sliced potatoes with the olive oil, bake seasoning, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt until coated.
Transfer the potatoes to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang, soy sauce, and a splash of water to create a glaze.
Brush the glaze evenly over the potatoes, then sprinkle with sesame seeds and chopped green onions.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until the potatoes are tender and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
